questions.q: If you overtake the person in 2nd place in a race, what place are you in?
If there are 3 apples and you take away 2, how many do you have?
What has keys but no locks?
If you have a bowl with six apples and you take away four, how many do you have?
What can you hold in your right hand but not in your left?
What gets wetter the more it dries?
A farmer has 17 sheep. All but 9 die. How many are left?
What word is spelled incorrectly in every dictionary?
Before Mount Everest was discovered, what was the tallest mountain?
What has 4 wheels and flies?
How many months have 28 days?
What comes once in a minute, twice in a moment, but never in a thousand years?
How many times can you subtract 5 from 25?
What has a head and a tail but no body?
A rooster lays an egg on top of a barn roof. Which way does it roll?
What has many teeth but cannot bite?
What has hands but cannot clap?
What goes up but never comes down?
If a red house is made of red bricks and a blue house is made of blue bricks, what is a greenhouse made of?
What has a neck but no head?
What kind of room has no doors or windows?
What can travel around the world while staying in one corner?
What has one eye but cannot see?
Two fathers and two sons go fishing. They catch exactly three fish, and each person takes home one fish. How is this possible?
choices: They are grandfather, father, and son

questions.q: What gets bigger the more you take away from it?
A man builds a rectangular house with all four walls facing south. A bear walks by. What color is the bear?
If two hours ago it was as long after one o'clock in the afternoon as it was before one o'clock in the morning, what time is it now?
A clock takes 6 seconds to strike 4 o'clock. How many seconds does it take to strike 8 o'clock?
If it takes 5 machines 5 minutes to make 5 widgets, how long would 100 machines take to make 100 widgets?
In a lake, a patch of lily pads doubles in size every day. If it takes 48 days to cover the entire lake, how long does it take to cover half the lake?
A snail climbs a 10-foot well, going up 3 feet each day but slipping back 2 feet each night. On which day does it reach the top?
A bat and a ball cost $1.10 in total. The bat costs $1.00 more than the ball. How much does the ball cost?
You see a boat full of people on a river, yet there isn't a single person on board. How is that possible?
A girl has as many brothers as sisters, but each brother has only half as many brothers as sisters. How many brothers and sisters are in the family?
You have two ropes that each take exactly one hour to burn, but they burn unevenly. Using only these ropes and a lighter, how can you measure exactly 45 minutes?
choices: Burn one rope at both ends and the other at one end simultaneously, then light the second rope's other end when the first finishes
Cut one rope in half and burn it
Burn both ends of one rope, then light the other from both ends
Light one rope's middle and time the halves

questions.q: In a logic puzzle, there are knights who always tell the truth and knaves who always lie. A person says, 'I am a knave.' What are they?
choices: It is impossible for anyone to say this

questions.q: You have 8 identical-looking balls; 7 weigh the same and one is heavier. Using a balance scale, what is the minimum number of weighings guaranteed to find the heavy ball?

A monk leaves at dawn and walks up a mountain, arriving at dusk. The next day at dawn he walks back down the same path, arriving at dusk. Must there be a point on the path he occupies at exactly the same time of day on both trips?
choices: Only if he walks at constant speed

questions.q: In the classic puzzle, you have three boxes labeled 'Apples', 'Oranges', and 'Apples and Oranges', and every label is wrong. What is the minimum number of fruits you must draw to correctly relabel all three boxes?
What is the next number in the sequence 1, 11, 21, 1211, 111221, ...?
Using a 5-liter jug and a 3-liter jug with no other markings, what is the largest exact amount you cannot measure out into one of the jugs?
choices: There is no amount from 0 to 5 you cannot measure

questions.q: A frog falls into a 30-foot well. Each day it climbs up 3 feet and each night slides down 2 feet. Starting from the bottom, on which day does it escape?
If five cats can catch five mice in five minutes, how many cats are needed to catch 100 mice in 100 minutes?
In the river-crossing puzzle, a farmer must take a wolf, a goat, and a cabbage across a river in a boat that holds only him plus one item. What is the minimum number of river crossings required?
